pericardium.physics: Thin translucent membrane, glistening serous surface, complex folding at arterial/venous reflections

heart_internal.textures: Columnar carnous trabeculae, fibrous chordae tendineae, smooth endocardial lining
heart_internal.physics: Muscular red-brown tones, high specular on valve leaflets

heart_external_features.shape: Trapezoidal/Pyramidal with apex directed anteriorly/left, base posteriorly
heart_external_features.physics: Myocardial texture, epicardial fat on sulci, glistening serous pericardial reflection

anastomoses: LAD hooks around apex to meet Posterior IV branch

landmarks: Cardiac crux (intersection of coronary and posterior IV sulci)
coronary_circulation.physics: Branching pattern determines cardiac dominance; most people are Right dominant (RCA gives Posterior IV artery). Arterial tunics: Adventitia, Media, Intima.
coronary_circulation.visual_rules: Coronary arteries are embedded in epicardial fat within sulci. LAD must be shown as the 'widowmaker' in anterior view.

arterial: Right PA passes UNDER arch of aorta
Arteries paired with bronchial tree path
venous: Carry oxygenated blood to Left Atrium
Right superior PV receives Middle lobe vein

cardiac_cycle.diastole: Ventricular relaxation, filling (80% passive, 20% atrial contraction), closure of aortic/pulmonary valves, opening of tricuspid/mitral valves
cardiac_cycle.systole: Ventricular contraction, ejection, closure of tricuspid/mitral valves, opening of aortic/pulmonary valves

systemic_and_pulmonary_circulation.physics: Continuous loop with pumps in series, parallel delivery mapping, high-pressure arterial vs low-pressure venous.
systemic_and_pulmonary_circulation.visual_rules: Pulmonary arteries must be blue (deoxygenated) and pulmonary veins red (oxygenated). Systemic circulation operates in parallel to organs.

axillary_artery_and_branches.course: Continuation of subclavian artery at outer border of 1st rib; becomes brachial artery at lower border of teres major.
second_part: Thoraco-acromial artery (4 branches: Acromial, Deltoid, Pectoral, Clavicular)
axillary_artery_and_branches.physics: Arterial pulsatility, collateral flow around scapula if ligated
axillary_artery_and_branches.visual_rules: Posterior circumflex humeral artery must be shown passing through the quadrangular space with the axillary nerve.

superior_vessels: Profunda brachii artery (Middle collateral, Radial collateral)
periarticular_arterial_anastomoses_elbow.anastomotic_logic: Collateral circulation allows blood to reach the forearm even when the elbow is flexed.

hand_arches: Common and proper palmar digital arteries
forearm_and_hand_arterial_circuits.physics: Dual supply via arches ensures distal perfusion; pinch-test collateral check (Allen Test logic)

venous_canals_arm_and_forearm.physics: Low-pressure flow, visible valves in larger veins, superficial accessibility for venepuncture
venous_canals_arm_and_forearm.visual_rules: Median cubital vein should be shown crossing the bicipital aponeurosis, which separates it from the underlying brachial artery.

deep_venous_drainage_upper_limb.drainage_logic: Deep veins run in pairs alongside corresponding arteries, receiving muscular tributaries.

sino_atrial_SA_node: Location: Junction of SVC and RA, superior end of sulcus terminalis
Supplied by SA nodal artery (usually RCA branch)
atrio_ventricular_AV_node: Location: Postero-inferior part of interatrial septum

structures: Septomarginal trabecula (Moderator band - carries right bundle to anterior papillary muscle)
cardiac_conduction_system.physics: Myogenic initiation at SA node (~70 bpm), nodal delay at AV node, rapid conduction through Purkinje network
cardiac_conduction_system.visual_rules: Conducting tissue should be shown as a glowing or distinct yellow-colored pathway within the myocardium.

atherosclerosis_stages: Mid: Fibrous plaque (smooth muscle proliferation, collagen cap)
Advanced: Complicated plaque (calcification, hemorrhage, ulceration, thrombosis)

angioplasty: Flattening of plaque against vessel wall

cardiovascular_pathology_and_interventio.physics: Lumen narrowing (stenosis) increases shear stress; balloon inflation requires high-fidelity mechanical expansion mapping.

branches: Brachiocephalic trunk (splits into R subclavian and R common carotid)
neighboring_nerves: Right Vagus (passes ANTERIOR to R subclavian)
Right Recurrent Laryngeal (hooks UNDER R subclavian)
Left Vagus (passes ANTERIOR to Aortic Arch)
Left Recurrent Laryngeal (hooks UNDER Aortic Arch/Ligamentum arteriosum)

tributaries: Left suprarenal vein (to Left Renal Vein)
Right/Left renal veins (Left is longer, crosses aorta)
Left gonadal vein (to Left Renal Vein)
connections: Ascending lumbar veins (connect to Azygos/Hemiazygos system)
Median sacral vein (to L common iliac)
systemic_veins_ivc.asymmetry: IVC is to the RIGHT of midline; Left vessels (renal, suprarenal, gonadal) must travel further to reach it or drain into the Left Renal Vein.
systemic_veins_ivc.physics: Thin-walled, low-pressure, large-diameter blue structures; valvular presence in iliacs but mostly absent in IVC main trunk.

superficial_veins.great_saphenous: Medial side of foot/leg/thigh; drains to femoral vein at saphenous opening
superficial_veins.small_saphenous: Lateral side of foot, posterior leg; drains to popliteal vein
superficial_veins.dorsal_venous_arch: Source of saphenous veins on foot

lower_limb_venous_system.valves: Essential bicuspid valves for anti-gravity return; spacing every 2-5 cm
lower_limb_venous_system.physics: Dark blue deoxygenated aesthetic, volumetric distension in superficial vessels, high-contrast valves

EJV_formation: Posterior division of Retromandibular + Posterior auricular -> External Jugular (EJV)
superficial_venous_mapping.IJV_source: Continuation of Sigmoid sinus at jugular foramen; descends in carotid sheath; joins subclavian to form Brachiocephalic vein at T1 level.
superficial_venous_mapping.asymmetry_and_valves: Valves located at termination of EJV and IJV to prevent reflux from brachiocephalic veins.

venous_anastomoses_clinical.danger_triangle_of_face: Angular/Ophthalmic veins -> Cavernous Sinus connection; risk of intracranial infection (thrombosis)
venous_anastomoses_clinical.facial_IJV_link: Anterior division of retromandibular + Facial vein -> Internal Jugular (IJV)

head_and_neck_venous_drainage.physics: Retrograde flow permitted in valve-less facial veins; EJV crosses SCM obliquely; IJV is deep to SCM.

neck_regions_and_clinical_triangles.sedillots_triangle: Between sternal/clavicular heads of SCM and clavicle; key landmark for IJV puncture.

clinical_access.subclavian_puncture: Index finger in jugular notch; needle inserted inferior to clavicle; vein is ANTERIOR to anterior scalene muscle.
clinical_access.IJV_puncture: Apex of Sedillot's triangle; lateral to carotid pulse; aimed toward ipsilateral nipple.
clinical_access.carotid_pulse: Carotid triangle; anterior to SCM at C4 level.

neck_regions_and_clinical_triangles.physics: Muscular tensioning shifts with head rotation; fascia layers (Investing, Pretracheal, Prevertebral) create potential spaces.

areas.aortic_area: 2nd Right Intercostal Space (RIC) at sternal border
areas.pulmonary_area: 2nd Left Intercostal Space (LIC) at sternal border
areas.tricuspid_area: 5th Left Intercostal Space (LIC) at sternal border
areas.mitral_area: 5th Left Intercostal Space (LIC) at Midclavicular line (Apex location)

surface_border_mapping.superior_border: Line connecting inferior border of 2nd LIC to superior border of 3rd RIC
surface_border_mapping.inferior_border: 6th RIC through xiphisternal joint to 5th LIC at MCL

cardiac_auscultation_surface_anatomy.physics: Sound projection follows blood flow downstream from valves; auscultation points are not necessarily directly over the valves.

cerebral_arterial_circle_of_willis.physics: Hexagonal redundancy (Collateral flow), high-fidelity arterial branching, clear vessel distinction at base of brain
cerebral_arterial_circle_of_willis.visual_rules: Posterior communicating artery must be shown connecting the ICA to the PCA to complete the circle.

bifurcation_atherosclerosis.location: Common Carotid bifurcation (Superior border of thyroid cartilage)
pathology: Turbulent flow markers at carotid sinus

carotid_vascular_pathology.physics: High-shear stress at bifurcation points, erratic flow vectors around plaque, lumen narrowing mapping.

spaces: Subarachnoid space (Contains CSF and cerebral vessels)

ventricular_system_and_csf_flow.physics: Pulsatile CSF flow, crystal-clear fluid transparency, high-contrast ependymal lining
ventricular_system_and_csf_flow.visual_rules: Flow vectors must show CSF moving from lateral ventricles down to the 4th ventricle and out into the subarachnoid space.

lacrimal_apparatus: Lacrimal canaliculi -> Lacrimal sac -> Nasolacrimal duct (Inferomedial)

features: Optic disc (Blind spot, source of vessels)

accommodation.distant_vision: Ciliary muscle relaxed, Zonular fibers TENSIONED, Lens thin
accommodation.near_vision: Ciliary muscle CONTRACTS (Parasympathetic), Zonular fibers relax, Lens thickens (spherical)

special_sensory_orbit_and_fundus.physics: Refractive lens transparency, retinal vessel branching (non-anastomosing), aqueous/vitreous humor clarity

cranial_nerve_exit_and_functional_mappin.cn_iii_oculomotor: Superior orbital fissure; Eye movement, pupil constriction
cranial_nerve_exit_and_functional_mappin.cn_iv_trochlear: Superior orbital fissure; superior oblique muscle

cn_v_trigeminal.v1_ophthalmic: Superior orbital fissure; Sensory to forehead/eye
cn_v_trigeminal.v2_maxillary: Foramen rotundum; Sensory to face/upper teeth
cn_v_trigeminal.v3_mandibular: Foramen ovale; Muscles of mastication, sensory to lower teeth/jaw

cranial_nerve_exit_and_functional_mappin.cn_vi_abducent: Superior orbital fissure; lateral rectus muscle
cranial_nerve_exit_and_functional_mappin.cn_vii_facial: Internal acoustic meatus -> stylomastoid foramen; facial expression
cranial_nerve_exit_and_functional_mappin.visual_rules: All nerves must be shown exiting corresponding foramina in the cranial base view.

middle_ear_ossicles.physics: Mechanical vibration conduction, pressure equalization via tube

ear_and_auditory_mechanics.physics: Bone-conduction stippling, fluid-filled membranous labyrinth, high-contrast nerve pathways
ear_and_auditory_mechanics.view: Anterior / Coronal section / Detailed internal ear

medulla: CN IX, X, XI: Lateral to Olive
CN XII: Medial to Olive (Pyramidal-Olivary groove)
jugular_foramen_contents: ICA is anterior in carotid canal

neuroanatomy_and_csf_dynamics.view: Median sagittal / Lateral / Superior / Transverse section

pharynx_divisions: Nasopharynx (Posterior to nose, above soft palate)
Oropharynx (Posterior to mouth, from soft palate to epiglottis)

superior_laryngeal_nerve: Internal branch (Sensory above vocal folds; pierces thyrohyoid membrane)
innervation.recurrent_laryngeal_nerve: Motor to all other intrinsic muscles; sensory below vocal folds; loops under aortic arch (Left) or subclavian (Right)

laryngeal_neurovascular_and_lymphatics.physics: High-fidelity nerve branching, mucosal sheen, lymphatic node stippling

upper_respiratory_and_digestive_crossove.airway_food_crossing: Airway is Anterior (Trachea); Food pathway is Posterior (Esophagus). Epiglottis acts as a valve.
upper_respiratory_and_digestive_crossove.vasculature_palate: Greater/Lesser palatine arteries (from Maxillary); drains to Pterygoid venous plexus.
upper_respiratory_and_digestive_crossove.physics: Mucosal sheen, muscular peristalsis initialization, high-fidelity tongue stippling, wet specular reflections
upper_respiratory_and_digestive_crossove.view: Median sagittal / Open mouth anterior / Superior (palate)

features: Right lung (3 lobes: Superior, Middle, Inferior; Oblique/Horizontal fissures)
Left lung (2 lobes: Superior, Inferior; Oblique fissure)
neighboring_nerves: Left recurrent laryngeal nerve (loops under arch of aorta)
lungs_macro.textures: Spongy, porous texture, mottled pinkish-gray, cartilaginous rings, glistening serous membranes

surface_projections.apical_standard: Pleura/Apex extends above clavicle into supraclavicular fossa (C7 level)

clinical_landmarks: Bare area of pericardium (accessible puncture site)
pleural_spaces_and_reflects.physics: Kidney-shaped thoracic cavity in cross-section due to protruding vertebral bodies; contains 2 pulmonary cavities and 1 mediastinum.
pleural_spaces_and_reflects.view: Anterior / Posterior surface mapping / Transverse CT

micro_alveolar.vascular_pairing: Pulmonary arteries branch in unison with bronchi (paired); Pulmonary veins are intersegmental/between segments.
micro_alveolar.physics: Highly delicate membrane transparency, gas exchange interface, 2D/3D mesh of capillaries, surfactant-lined inner surface
micro_alveolar.visual_rules: Type II cells appear larger and rounded/blue-ish, nestled in the alveolar corners; Type I cells are thin and line the walls.

bronchial_tree_hierarchy.physics: Progressive decrease in cartilage, increase in smooth muscle distal to bronchi.

upper_gastrointestinal.textures: Mucosal rugae in stomach, glistening muscularis, granular salivary gland texture
upper_gastrointestinal.physics: Subsurface scattering of mucosa, high specular on gastric secretions, peristaltic wave propagation mapping

esophagus_details.view: Left lateral / Transverse section / Coronal section

abdominal_viscera_peritoneum.physics: Serous fluid lubrication, fat storage in greater omentum (the 'policeman' of the abdomen).

abdominal_vasculature_unpaired.physics: Arterial supply corresponds to embryological gut divisions; Portal system drains majority of GI tract to liver for processing.
abdominal_vasculature_unpaired.visual_rules: SMA must be shown passing anterior to the 3rd part of the duodenum and posterior to the pancreas neck.

boundaries.posterior: Transversalis fascia, reinforced medially by Inguinal falx (Conjoint tendon)
boundaries.roof: Arching fibers of internal oblique and transversus abdominis
boundaries.floor: Inguinal ligament (incurved edge of external oblique aponeurosis), Iliopubic tract

rings: Superficial inguinal ring (in external oblique aponeurosis)
inguinal_canal_anatomy.physics: Oblique pathway (~4cm) through abdominal wall to resist herniation; canal pressure increases during coughing/straining.

liver_and_biliary_system.textures: Smooth glistening capsule (Glisson's), high-fidelity venous branching, dense parenchymal texture

duodenum.2nd_descending: Retroperitoneal, Descends along R side of L2-L3, contains Major/Minor papillae
duodenum.3rd_horizontal: Crossed by SMA/SMV, anterior to IVC/Aorta
duodenum.4th_ascending: Duodenojejunal flexure (L2 level), Suspended by Ligament of Treitz

large_intestine_clinical.pathology: Diverticulosis (multiple outpouchings in sigmoid colon wall)
large_intestine_clinical.procedure: Colonoscopy (flexible fiberoptic tube insertion, visualization of mucosa/haustra)

pancreas_and_intestines.textures: Lobulated pancreatic texture, villous intestinal lining, haustra of colon, fatty appendices epiploicae
pancreas_and_intestines.view: Anterior abdominal dissection / Endoscopic clinical view

arteries: Celiac trunk (branches: Left gastric, Splenic, Common hepatic)
visceral_vasculature.physics: Thin-walled distended blue venous structures, high-contrast arterial branching, white glistening cord-like nerves

capillary_logic.physics: Transition from high-pressure red oxygenated blood to low-pressure blue deoxygenated blood within a web-like interconnected mesh.
capillary_logic.distribution: Interstitium and organ-specific beds (like the pulmonary/renal plexuses).

visceral_afferent_pain.referred_pain_logic: Visceral pain signals traveling alongside sympathetic fibers to specific spinal cord segments; manifests as referred pain in dermatomes.

landmarks: Iliac crest (1 finger below R kidney)
kidney_macro_posterior.displacement: Moves 2-3 cm vertically during deep breathing/diaphragm descent
kidney_macro_posterior.textures: Smooth fibrous capsule, dense muscular background
kidney_macro_posterior.physics: Muted skin tones with deep internal organ silhouette transparency, respiration-driven vertical oscillation

kidney_internal_micro.textures: Granulated cortex, striated medullary pyramids, high-specular renal pelvis lining, fibromuscular hilum

structures: Nephron loop (Loop of Henle - descending/ascending thin limbs)
Collecting duct (Cortical, Outer medullary, Inner medullary)
microvasculature: Vasa recta network (parallel to Loop of Henle)
nephron_and_glomerulus.physics: Functional flow indicators, ADH-controlled water resorption, countercurrent exchange, selective secretion, high-fidelity filtration barrier, microvascular wrapping
nephron_and_glomerulus.visual_rules: The Nephron should be shown wrapped in a dense red/blue capillary plexus. Capillaries should appear thin and intertwined with the tubule structures.

renal_ultrastructure_em.textures: Nanometer-scale texture, proteinaceous diaphragm, basement membrane density

morphology.right_gland: Pyramidal shape, sits atop kidney pole
morphology.left_gland: Semilunar/Crescent shape, extends down medial border

veins: Left suprarenal vein (to Left Renal Vein)

suprarenal_adrenal_glands.physics: Yellowish glandular texture, extremely high metabolic vascularity, endocrine-driven hierarchical branching

congenital_anomalies.horseshoe_kidney: Inferior poles fused, U-shaped, trapped under IMA at L3-L5
congenital_anomalies.ectopic_pelvic_kidney: In embryonic pelvic position, anterior to sacrum
congenital_anomalies.retrocaval_ureter: Ureter loops posterior to the IVC

renal_anomalies_and_calculi.physics: Crystalline mineral density, obstructive pressure flow, referred dermatomal pain mapping

urinary_bladder.morphology: Apex, Body, Fundus, Neck, Detrusor muscle
urinary_bladder.physics: Expandable volume; superior position when distended increases rupture risk from abdominal injury.

urinary_tract_system.textures: Translucent muscular ureteral tubes, thick corrugated detrusor muscle wall

male_reproductive_genital.textures: Glandular parenchyma, coiled ductal networks, dense fibrous capsule, erectile tissue hydraulics

female_reproductive_genital.physics: Cyclical mucosal changes, distensible muscular walls, ligamentous support tensioning

skin_layers_and_specialized_structures.physics: Subsurface scattering for South Asian skin tones, high specular on sweat secretions, matte fibrous dermis, translucent fatty tissue
skin_layers_and_specialized_structures.visual_rules: The Epidermis must be shown as avascular; all vessels and nerve terminals are located in the dermis or subcutaneous layers. Skin ligaments must appear as fibrous bands anchoring the dermis to the deep fascia.

femur_mechanics.head: Fovea for ligament
femur_mechanics.neck_angles: Adult: 126°, Old age: 120°, Child: 135° (Angle of inclination)

axial_and_appendicular_skeleton.physics: Aged ivory bone texture, hard cortical stippling, clear marrow cavity in sectioned views

mandible_features.body: Base, Alveolar part (teeth sockets), Mental foramen/protuberance

pathology_fractures: Basilar (base involvement: foramen magnum, occipital condyles)
cranial_and_facial_architecture.physics: Bone density stippling, jagged suture interlocking, thin bone at pterion (clinical risk for MMA hemorrhage); facial muscles are superficial and lack deep fascia.

gluteal_region.sciatic_nerve_rel: Enters gluteal region through greater sciatic foramen, usually inferior to piriformis.

contents: Sciatic nerve bifurcation (Tibial and Common Fibular nerves)
popliteal_fossa.visual_rules: Tibial nerve is most superficial, Popliteal artery is deepest. Dissection from medial side avoids tibial motor branches.

vasculature_distal.dorsal: Anterior Tibial -> Dorsalis Pedis (lateral to EHL tendon)
vasculature_distal.plantar: Posterior Tibial -> Medial/Lateral Plantar arteries -> Deep Plantar Arch

fascia.superficial: Loose connective tissue, contains fat, Great/Small saphenous veins, cutaneous nerves

lower_limb_regional_and_fascial_mapping.physics: Tight compartmental pressure zones, glistening fascial sheaths, high-fidelity muscular definition

knee_joint_integrity.physics: Glistening white fibrocartilage, crossing cruciate geometry, 'bow-tie' meniscus in MRI coronal plane
knee_joint_integrity.view: Superior (tibia) / Posterior / Coronal section / MRI correlation

kinesiology_and_gait.physics: Weight-bearing line (gravity) shifting, double vs. single support logic, multi-joint coordination

quadrants: LUQ (Stomach, Spleen, L Kidney, Pancreas body)

Horizontal planes: Transtubercular plane (L5 tubercle of iliac crest)

viscera_in_situ_orientation.diaphragm_domes: Right dome (4th ICS) higher than Left dome (5th ICS) due to Liver size.

abdominopelvic_cavity_architecture.physics: Intra-abdominal pressure mapping; muscular floor and roof permit expulsion dynamics (Valsalva reflex).

hip_bones: Ilium (Ala, Body, Iliac crest, ASIS, AIIS)

pelvic_cavity_divisions.greater_false_pelvis: Superior to pelvic brim (abdominal viscera protection)
pelvic_cavity_divisions.lesser_true_pelvis: Between pelvic inlet (brim) and outlet; contains pelvic organs
pelvic_cavity_divisions.pelvic_brim_inlet: Line connecting sacral promontory, arcuate line of ilium, and pectineal line of pubis

pelvic_architecture.physics: Stress distribution through SI joints to femurs; pelvic floor acts as a dynamic tensioned hammock.

pivot: Permit rotation (rounded process in bony/ligamentous socket)

synovial_joint_classifications.physics: Low-friction glistening cartilage surfaces, viscous fluid transparency, blue articular cartilage glow

features: Sternal angle (Angle of Louis at T4-T5 level)
ribcage_osteology.textures: Hard cortical bone finish, translucent/smooth costal cartilage
ribcage_osteology.physics: Resilient osteocartilaginous cage protects thoracic and upper abdominal organs; limited anterior mobility vs greater lateral/posterior stability.

thoracic_musculature.textures: Striated muscle fibers, thin glistening fascia layers
thoracic_musculature.physics: Muscle red-brown tonal shifts, high specular on fascia reflections
thoracic_musculature.identity_standard: South Asian muscular morphology, modern clinical lighting

skeletal_muscle_architecture.physics: Fiber arrangement dictates contraction force and range of motion; pennate muscles generate higher force, parallel muscles permit greater shortening.
skeletal_muscle_architecture.visual_rules: Muscle fibers must clearly follow the architectural direction (e.g., feather-like for pennate, fan-like for convergent).

superficial_muscular_system.identity_standard: South Asian muscular morphology, anatomically precise biomechanical posture
superficial_muscular_system.anatomy_mapping: Global superficial map for locomotion and posture.

motor_unit_components: Motor neuron cell body (in spinal cord)
muscle_ultrastructure_and_motor_unit.physics: Sliding filament theory (Actin/Myosin interdigitation), discrete unit recruitment for graded force, visible striations from sarcomere alignment
muscle_ultrastructure_and_motor_unit.visual_rules: Motor end plates should appear as synaptic terminals branching onto the sarcolemma. Connective tissue layers must be distinct and transparent/white to contrast the red muscle fibers.

regions: Cervical (7): Atlas C1, Axis C2 with Dens
vertebral_column.physics: Load-bearing S-curve, shock absorption via IV discs, protection of spinal cord.

hand: Carpal bones (Scaphoid/Trapezium tubercles, Pisiform, Hook of hamate)

biomechanics_clavicle_fracture.mechanism: Medial fragment pulled SUPERIORLY by sternocleidomastoid. Lateral fragment pulled INFERIORLY by gravity and MEDIALLY by pectoralis major (causing overriding).
biomechanics_clavicle_fracture.vulnerable_structures: Brachial plexus trunks, Subclavian vessels, Coracoclavicular ligament.

upper_limb_osteology_and_biomechanics.physics: Rotational freedom at the shoulder, complex hinge/pivot at the elbow, precise multiaxial hinging in digits.

pectoralis_major: Attaches to lateral lip of intertubercular sulcus
pectoralis_minor: Anchors to coracoid process of scapula
serratus_anterior: Attaches along lateral ribcage (Ribs 1-9) to medial border of scapula (anterior side)
anterior_axio_appendicular_muscles.physics: Moves the pectoral girdle and upper limb. Pectoralis major is a prime mover for adduction and medial rotation.

scapulohumeral_muscles_and_rotator_cuff.physics: Rotator cuff muscles fuse with the joint capsule to form a musculotendinous cuff, stabilizing the humeral head within the shallow glenoid cavity.

boundaries.apex: Cervico-axillary canal (Clavicle, 1st rib, Scapula superior edge)
boundaries.posterior_wall: Scapula, Subscapularis, Teres major, Latissimus dorsi

axilla_anatomy_and_boundaries.physics: Pyramidal distribution center for neurovascular structures passing to the upper limb.

muscles: Biceps brachii (Long head: supraglenoid tubercle; Short head: coracoid process)
anterior_flexor_compartment.action: Flexion of arm and forearm, Supination (Biceps)

muscles: Triceps brachii (Long head: infraglenoid tubercle; Lateral/Medial head: humerus)

muscles_of_the_arm_compartments.physics: Intermuscular septa divide the arm into functional compartments.
muscles_of_the_arm_compartments.visual_rules: The long head of triceps must be shown separating the quadrangular and triangular spaces.

contents: Brachial artery (bifurcating into Radial and Ulnar)
cubital_fossa_anatomy.physics: Transition zone from arm to forearm; neurovascular structures are protected by the bicipital aponeurosis.

posterior_extensor_supinator_compartment.nerve: Deep branch of radial nerve (Posterior interosseous nerve)

forearm_musculature_compartments.physics: Compartments separated by radius, ulna, and interosseous membrane; cross-sectional circularity vs flattened distal profile
forearm_musculature_compartments.visual_rules: The common extensor tendon originates from the lateral epicondyle. The outcropping muscles of the thumb must be shown crossing over the radial extensors.

fascia_of_the_upper_limb_and_hand.physics: Fascial thickening forms pulleys (retinacula) to prevent tendon bowstringing.
fascia_of_the_upper_limb_and_hand.visual_rules: Bicipital aponeurosis must be shown as a thin fibrous slip crossing the cubital fossa.

drainage_ducts: Right lymphatic duct (drains upper right quadrant to right venous angle)
Thoracic duct (drains remainder of body into left venous angle)
lymphatic_drainage_pattern.physics: Unidirectional flow driven by valves and surrounding muscle pumps, converging from capillaries to lymphatic venous angles.

axillary_lymph_node_hierarchy.drainage_logic: Breast tissue drains predominantly to pectoral nodes. Upper limb drains to humeral and central nodes. Flow converges centrally then apically before entering venous circulation.

cortex: B cells in primary lymphoid follicles

lymph_node_architecture.textures: Dense lymphoid follicles with pale germinal centres, sinusoidal networks, smooth fibrous capsule
lymph_node_architecture.physics: Lymphatic flow filters through sinuses, high metabolic activity in follicles during antigen exposure
lymph_node_architecture.view: Cross-section of a lymph node / Microscopic view

immune_cell_catalog.morphology: Varied nuclear shapes (multilobed, kidney-shaped, bilobed), specific cytoplasmic granulation patterns
immune_cell_catalog.physics: Dynamic amoeboid movement, receptor-mediated antigen recognition

antibody_structure.physics: Y-shaped bifunctional molecule, high-affinity binding sites

phagocytosis_and_signaling.physics: Signal transduction from membrane to nucleus, enzymatic digestion within lysosomes

phases: Sensitization phase (Allergen → T/B cell activation → IgE production)
Binding phase (IgE binds to Fc receptors on Mast cells)
hypersensitivity_type_1.physics: Immediate degranulation, granular release into extracellular space, rapid vascular response

human_microbiome_flora.textures: Microbial colonies, biofilm-like layering, fungal budding (Candida)

surgical_prosthetic: Surgical site infection (Staph. aureus, Strep, Anaerobes)

healthcare_associated_infections.identity_standard: Indian female subject, modern clinical environment (ICU/Ward), showing medical devices and associated localized inflammation (erythematous borders)
healthcare_associated_infections.physics: Biofilm formation on medical plastics, purulent exudate at insertion sites

steps: Right palm over left dotsum with interlaced fingers
Palm to palm with fingers interlaced
Backs of fingers to opposing palms
clinical_hygiene_protocols.physics: Mechanical friction, surfactant action, removal of transient flora

clinical_critical_care.view: Multifaceted clinical monitoring map / Ghosted anatomy with equipment overlay

clinical_examination_cancer.identity_standard: Indian subject (male or female), showing visible signs of clinical metabolic stress (cachexia), South Asian skin tones with specific toxicity manifestations (hyperpigmentation, inflammation)
clinical_examination_cancer.physics: Subsurface scattering of wasting tissue, dull skin luster in dehydration, specific inflammatory glows at toxicity sites

head_and_neck: Angioedema of lips and mucous membranes
anaphylaxis_systemic.identity_standard: Indian subject, warm South Asian skin tones, presenting with multi-systemic acute distress markers (facial swelling, urticarial wheals), modern clinical or emergency setting
anaphylaxis_systemic.physics: Systemic capillary leak, massive vasodilatory response, metabolic crash
anaphylaxis_systemic.view: Full-body systemic map / Disease overlay on ghost silhouette

sepsis_pathophysiology.physics: Impaired oxygen delivery-consumption balance, microcirculatory shunting, mitochondrial hibernation
sepsis_pathophysiology.view: Triad of scales: Macro / Micro / Cellular

acute_inflammation_systemic.identity_standard: Indian male subject, warm South Asian skin tones, authentic features showing fatigue and febrile flushing, modern Indian clinical setting

acute_inflammation_local.textures: Edematous skin (swollen/tense), erythematous streaks in lymphangitis, purulent zones

anatomical_planes.median_plane: Vertical plane dividing body into equal right/left halves.
anatomical_planes.sagittal_planes: Vertical planes parallel to the median plane.
anatomical_planes.frontal_coronal_planes: Vertical planes dividing body into anterior (ventral) and posterior (dorsal).
anatomical_planes.transverse_axial_planes: Horizontal planes dividing body into superior (cranial) and inferior (caudal).

pns: Spinal nerves (31 pairs: Cervical, Thoracic, Lumbar, Sacral, Coccygeal)
central_and_peripheral_organization.physics: Integration and coordination of neural signals centrally, transmission peripherally.

somatic_pathway: Somatic motor fiber (from anterior horn to skeletal muscle)
visceral_sympathetic_pathway: Presynaptic fiber (from lateral horn to sympathetic ganglion)
somatic_and_visceral_innervation.physics: Somatic permits voluntary/reflexive movement; Visceral controls involuntary functions. Distinct gray matter horns for different pathways.

neuron_typology.physics: Direction of conduction flows from dendrites/receptors to axon terminals. Myelin sheath insulates for rapid saltatory conduction.

neurological_cortex.physics: Columnar neuro-architecture, neurovascular coupling, synaptic density scaling

nerves: Spinal sensory ganglia in dural sleeves
spinal_cord_and_meninges.physics: Suspension in CSF, flexibility inside vertebral canal, termination of solid cord at L1-L2 transitioning to cauda equina.

radial_nerve_branching_and_course.pathway: Passes anterior to lateral epicondyle between brachialis and brachioradialis; divides in cubital fossa.
radial_nerve_branching_and_course.superficial_branch: Sensory branch running under cover of brachioradialis to dorsum of hand.
radial_nerve_branching_and_course.deep_branch: Motor branch penetrating the Supinator muscle to become the Posterior Interosseous Nerve (PIN).
radial_nerve_branching_and_course.physics: PIN lies between superficial and deep extensor layers, running with the posterior interosseous artery.

advanced_vascular_tunics.physics: Elastic recoil, pulsatile flow propagation, luminal shear stress
advanced_vascular_tunics.visual_rules: Adventitia must contain vasa vasorum in large vessels; media must show concentric smooth muscle layers.

NATURE.borders: Hidden or extremely fine vector paths

LANCET.borders: Clear black outlines for diagrammatic clarity
